flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: Case 6b (unsalvageable conjecture, stage_0.5 reject fast-path): reviewer's novelty verdict is `reject` with `proposal_promise_gap: tier_genuinely_below` — the headline equivalence `h_LP = h_eta iff eta >= eta_w*(P)` is a near-immediate support-function unfold of the very definition of `eta_w*(P)` (first radius at which the LP maximizer set meets `Q_eta(P)`), so the kernel is definitionally tautological rather than a flagship equivalence frontier between named LP and non-Gaussian SVAR estimators. Compounded by cluster anchor drift (locked block declares PartialID support-function anchor while run metadata + Lean target are `exactid`), the .tex is not salvageable as the same conjecture — re-deriving on the existing statement would burn Stage 0 budget on a sub-flagship kernel. Not a derivation slip (would be `stage_0`); not a localized typo (would be `stage_4d`); not assumption strengthening (no Bucket A signal).

proposed_from.topic: Sharp non-equivalence theorem for local-projection (LP) versus structural-vector-autoregression (SVAR) identification of structural impulse responses under non-Gaussian innovations and partial sign restrictions. Plagborg-Moller and Wolf (2021, Econometrica) established that LP and SVAR estimate the same population impulse response under finite-order linear VAR data-generating processes when both use the same identifying restrictions; Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf (Econometrica, forthcoming) extend the equivalence to local projections under misspecification of lag length. The flagship question: characterize the sharp boundary at which LP and SVAR identify distinct structural impulse responses when innovations are non-Gaussian with bounded higher-cumulant restrictions a la Gourieroux-Monfort-Renne (2017) and Lanne-Meitz-Saikkonen (2017), and when the structural impact matrix is restricted only by a partial sign/zero pattern. The kernel claim is a closed-form algebraic non-equivalence theorem: under non-Gaussian innovations with bounded fourth cumulant gap, the LP-IRF and SVAR-IRF identify distinct functionals of the structural-shock distribution unless a cumulant-matching restriction is jointly imposed, recovering Plagborg-Moller-Wolf equivalence at the Gaussian limit.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art is the banked parent eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1, whose proposed kernel handled eta=0 independent-shock cumulant diagonalization and whose derivation collapsed to a conditional margin theorem. This revision keeps the parent as the corner case but upgrades the assumption class to eta-bounded off-diagonal co-kurtosis leakage and makes the flagship delta the nondefinitional promotion from active-set rank certificates to global support equality frontier eta_w^dagger(P)=eta_w^*(P).","published_axis":"Closest published results are PlagborgMollerWolf2021 on common LP/VAR restrictions, GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 and LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 on independent non-Gaussian SVAR identification, LanneLiuLuoto2026 on dependent non-Gaussian shocks under zero relevant co-moments, and HoeschLeeMesters2024 on local-to-Gaussian robust inference. None gives a bounded nonzero co-kurtosis leakage frontier comparing LP/sign support with the same higher-cumulant SVAR shock label."}
seed_list: Parent v1 predicts exact independent cumulant matching is the LP/SVAR boundary; this upgrade conjectures a critical leakage radius eta_w^*(P) because bounded co-kurtosis dependence can restore equality only after exposed LP support contact.

PMW2021 would imply LP and VAR agree under common restrictions; this upgrade conjectures non-equivalence below eta_w^*(P) because LP sign restrictions omit the higher-cumulant contact condition used to label the same shock.

LanneLiuLuoto2026 shows dependent non-Gaussian shocks can still identify shocks under co-moment restrictions; this upgrade conjectures that the LP/SVAR equality frontier is the smallest co-kurtosis leakage needed to paste the LP support maximizer into that contact set.

seed_details.one_line: Parent v1 predicts exact independent cumulant matching is the LP/SVAR boundary; this upgrade conjectures a critical leakage radius eta_w^*(P) because bounded co-kurtosis dependence can restore equality only after exposed LP support contact.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith parent
anchor_paper.claim: the diagnostic margin chi_w(P) follows from fourth-cumulant geometry and partial sign restrictions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens independence premise, but not blocked: the weakening exposes a named phase-transition quantity eta_w^*(P) and reduces to parent at eta=0.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the parent because eta just relaxes the cumulant constraint monotonically.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: monotonicity gives nesting only; the sharp iff equality frontier and critical radius are new and were absent from parent chi_w(P).

adversarial_referee.instance: Headline needs an operational quantity, not qualitative leakage robustness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: eta_w^*(P) is the focal scalar and appears in locked parameters and Conjecture 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sufficiency eta>=eta* is easy; strictness for eta<eta* may hide compactness/contact gaps.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture explicitly includes the iff and isolates regular-frontier assumptions for the converse.

adversarial_referee.instance: Parent was downgraded because D0 substituted a conditional margin theorem.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: This proposal does not reuse the substituted theorem; it targets the missing primitive frontier with a widened assumption class.

seed_details.one_line: PMW2021 would imply LP and VAR agree under common restrictions; this upgrade conjectures non-equivalence below eta_w^*(P) because LP sign restrictions omit the higher-cumulant contact condition used to label the same shock.

anchor_paper.author: Plagborg-Moller and Wolf
anchor_paper.claim: LPs and VARs estimate the same population impulse responses when the same identifying restrictions are imposed, including sign restrictions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Single-comparator equivalence risk, but not blocked: it adds GMR/LMS and LanneLiuLuoto as a comparator chain and names eta_w^*(P).
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from PMW because omitted restrictions always break common-restriction equivalence.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: PMW gives the common-restriction corner; the proposal characterizes the sharp leakage frontier for equality after weakening independence.

adversarial_referee.instance: PMW never claims sign-only LP equals non-Gaussian SVAR with omitted cumulants.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal says informed by PMW common-restriction logic, not that PMW made the omitted-cumulant claim.
Conjecture 1 is positioned against PMW plus GMR/LMS plus LanneLiuLuoto2026.

adversarial_referee.instance: eta>=eta* sufficiency is definitional without necessity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The nontrivial part is the strict eta<eta* direction under regular contact and nonempty SVAR feasibility.

seed_details.one_line: LanneLiuLuoto2026 shows dependent non-Gaussian shocks can still identify shocks under co-moment restrictions; this upgrade conjectures that the LP/SVAR equality frontier is the smallest co-kurtosis leakage needed to paste the LP support maximizer into that contact set.

anchor_paper.author: Lanne, Liu, and Luoto
anchor_paper.claim: Dependent non-Gaussian shocks can be identified under absence of relevant co-skewness or co-kurtosis conditions.
anchor_paper.location: The Econometrics Journal advance article utag002, abstract

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens independence premise, but not blocked: the new phenomenon is a phase transition in LP/SVAR equality as leakage crosses eta_w^*(P).
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from LanneLiuLuoto2026 because they already handle dependent shocks.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: they study SVAR identification, not LP/sign support equality or the critical leakage radius for LP support contact.

adversarial_referee.instance: The 2026 paper may already have the co-kurtosis contact condition.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It identifies shocks under co-moment restrictions; it does not compare LP support maximizers to SVAR contact sets.

adversarial_referee.instance: A referee may ask why only one direction w, not all IRF reports jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The support-function formulation is directional by design; vector-set equality follows by quantifying over w in later strengthening.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs a computable map for eta_w^*(P).
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 2 supplies the two-shock closed-form residual; general case is a finite semialgebraic program.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 and parent map reused: Jorda2005 introduced LP IRFs; PlagborgMollerWolf2021 prove LP/VAR population IRF equivalence under common identifying restrictions including signs; MontielOleaPlagborgMoller2021 and MontielOleaPlagborgMollerQianWolf2024 motivate robust LP rows but do not add higher-cumulant labels; GourierouxMonfortRenne2017 and LanneMeitzSaikkonen2017 identify non-Gaussian SVAR shocks under independence; Guay2021 and LanneLuoto2021 give higher-moment/GMM identification tools; DrautzburgWright2023 refine sign-restricted VAR sets through independence and non-convex sets; HoeschLeeMesters2024 adds the frontier anchor on locally robust inference near local-to-Gaussian non-Gaussianity; LanneLiuLuoto2026 adds the generalization frontier allowing potentially dependent non-Gaussian shocks under co-moment restrictions. Verified web anchors: PMW2021, DrautzburgWright2023, HoeschLeeMesters2024, MOPQ2022, and LanneLiuLuoto2026.

banked_reason: Flagship-generalization upgrade D0.5 REJECT (Case 6b unsalvageable conjecture): D-0.5 reached ACCEPT@flagship+clean_substance 4 times across angles 0/1/2/3; D0 honestly confirmed both conjectures (conj_1_verdict=confirmed, conj_2_verdict=confirmed); D0.5 rejected with proposal_promise_gap:tier_genuinely_below because the headline iff 'h_LP = h_eta iff eta >= eta_w*(P)' is a near-immediate support-function unfold of the very definition of eta_w*(P) — definitionally tautological, not a flagship frontier. Combined with the prior mechanism upgrade (D-0.5 NO-PASS, persistent C-* well-posedness flags on flagship drafts) and the original v1 + D0-retest (kernel_substituted both times), this kernel empirically tops out at field tier; the flagship framing of the LP-vs-single-law-non-Gaussian-SVAR strict-gap is structurally either tautological or refuted at the open-class scope. Banking decision: retire eid_lp_svar_nonequiv from flagship pursuit.
